title: Capability-spec generator

expectedOutput: >-
  Markdown capability spec with functional requirements, performance thresholds,
  environmental constraints, and traceability back to the scenario.

  - operational-scenario-generator
planeContext:
  inputs:
    - what: Operational scenario for this capability

        Work item under `Knowledge Base/{{track}}/Operational scenarios/*`. Ask

    - what: Capability brief or statement of operational need
      description: >-
        Work items under *Briefs*, *Capability*, *Mission* (or with
        'capability-brief', 'operational-need', 'mission-statement' in titles).
    - what: >-
        Known constraints (optional) — regulatory, integration, schedule,
        platform
      description: >-
        Work items under *Constraints*, *Compliance*, *Architecture* (or with
        'constraints', 'regulatory', 'integration' in titles).
  outputPathTemplate: 'Knowledge Base/{{track}}/Capability specs/{{capability-name}}'
visibility: public

copyBlock: Use CLARA's `capability-spec-generator` for <programme name>.
---

## What CLARA will ask you for

- **Programme name** — your programme's name (e.g. SKYPROTECT).
- **Track** — the slice of the programme this artefact belongs to (workstream, capability area, feature line, etc.), or `Programme-wide` if it spans tracks.
- **Operational scenario** — page reference under `Knowledge Base/{{track}}/Operational scenarios/*` to base the spec on.
- **Capability name** — short (e.g. "Tank-crew alerting aid", "Fighter aircraft sensor-fusion module", "Frigate surface-contact classifier").
- **Inputs** — CLARA will search the programme's Plane project for operational scenario for this capability; capability brief or statement of operational need; known constraints (optional) — regulatory, integration, schedule, platform. You can also paste fresh material if it's not in Plane yet.

## Where the output lands

`Knowledge Base/{{track}}/Capability specs/{{capability-name}}` inside your programme's Plane project.

## Tips

- When you review CLARA's spec, every requirement should trace back to a specific scenario beat. Challenge any that can't — that's where drift creeps in.
- Performance thresholds are where CLARA's draft will be vaguest unless your operational scenario contains hard numbers. Push for measurable thresholds ("within 5 seconds, 95th percentile") rather than "fast enough".
- Pair CLARA's output with a [Napkin AI](/tools/napkin-ai) system diagram so the requirements have a structural picture to anchor against.
